tasks.title: Secure password storage with scrypt hashing
tasks.description: Replace plaintext ADMIN_PASSWORD comparison with scrypt-based hashing. Create src/core/auth.js using Node.js built-in crypto.scryptSync (zero dependencies). Support both plaintext and hashed passwords for backward compatibility. Format: $scrypt$<salt>$<hash>. Auto-upgrade plaintext to hash on first successful login.
tasks.details: Files: src/core/auth.js (new), src/server.js (login + change-password + upgrade-password endpoints), public/admin.html (upgrade banner + change password form in Settings). Verification: Login with plaintext → see upgrade banner → upgrade → login with hash.

tasks.title: Multi .env selector with UI dropdown
tasks.description: Support multiple named .env files (production, staging, personal) with dropdown switching in admin UI. Convention: ENV_FILES=name:path,name2:path2 and ACTIVE_ENV=name in local .env. Backward compatible with existing EXTERNAL_ENV_PATH. Hot-reload on switch.
tasks.details: Files: src/core/config.js (getEnvFiles, setActiveEnv, addEnvFile, removeEnvFile), src/server.js (4 new API routes), public/admin.html (dropdown + add/remove form). Reuse existing selectEnvFile() file picker.

tasks.title: Management tab with provider health table
tasks.description: Rewrite healthCheck.js stub into functional HealthMonitor class. Add Management tab to admin UI with table showing: provider name, status badge, key counts, last check time, request count, avg response time, action buttons. Auto-refresh every 30s.
tasks.details: Files: src/core/healthCheck.js (rewrite), src/server.js (4 health API routes + instantiate HealthMonitor), public/admin.html (new Management tab). Data sources: config.providers, providerClients, key-history.json, logBuffer. Status: active/degraded/failed/disabled.

tasks.title: Telegram broadcast and Slack webhook notifications
tasks.description: Create unified Notifier hub with Telegram broadcast and Slack webhook channels. Add broadcastMessage() to existing telegramBot.js. Create slackNotifier.js using built-in https (zero deps). Wire notifications into health events and key failures. Admin UI for notification settings.
tasks.details: Files: src/core/notifier.js (new), src/core/slackNotifier.js (new), src/core/telegramBot.js (add broadcastMessage), src/server.js (instantiate Notifier, notification API routes, wire events), public/admin.html (Notifications section in Settings). Config: SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL, SLACK_NOTIFY_ON, TELEGRAM_NOTIFY_ON.

tasks.title: Improve multi-env selector UI
tasks.description: The multi-env dropdown exists in admin.html but needs UX improvements: better visual feedback on switch, confirmation dialog, show active env badge in header, support drag-drop reordering of env profiles.

tasks.title: Migrate admin password from .env to secure session storage
tasks.description: Currently the scrypt-hashed password is stored in .env file. Migrate to cookie-based session auth: store only the password hash in a local encrypted file (data/admin.hash) instead of .env. Keep backward compatibility with existing .env ADMIN_PASSWORD. On first run, detect .env password, migrate to hash file, remove from .env. Session cookies (httpOnly, secure) for auth state, not password storage.

tasks.title: Auto-recovery of exhausted keys
tasks.description: Periodically retest API keys that were rotated out due to rate limits (429/402/403). After a configurable cooldown period (default: 5 minutes, env: KEYPROXY_RECOVERY_COOLDOWN_SEC), the HealthMonitor should probe the key with a lightweight test request. If it succeeds, mark the key as recovered and re-enable it in the rotation pool. Wire recovery events into the Notifier system (key X recovered after N minutes). Show recovery status in admin UI Management tab.
tasks.details: Files: src/core/healthCheck.js (add recovery scan in check cycle), src/core/keyRotator.js (add recoverKey method), src/core/keyHistory.js (add recovery state tracking), src/server.js (recovery API endpoint), public/admin.html (recovery badge in key table). Config: KEYPROXY_RECOVERY_COOLDOWN_SEC=300, KEYPROXY_RECOVERY_ENABLED=true.
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Let a key get 429'd, 2) Wait cooldown period, 3) Verify key is auto-tested, 4) Verify key reappears in rotation pool

tasks.title: Usage analytics dashboard with cost estimation
tasks.description: Build a new Analytics tab in the admin panel that tracks request count, estimated token usage, average latency, and cost per key per provider. Persist daily aggregates to data/analytics.json. Include charts: requests over time (line), latency distribution (bar), cost breakdown per provider (pie), top keys by usage (table). Add cost estimation based on built-in provider pricing tables (OpenAI: $5/M tokens GPT-4o, Gemini: $1.25/M tokens, etc). Support date range filtering (7d, 30d, all).
tasks.details: Files: src/core/analytics.js (new — AnalyticsTracker class with persist/aggregate/query methods), src/server.js (4 analytics API routes), public/admin.html (new Analytics tab with charts using Canvas API or lightweight chart lib). Pricing tables in src/core/pricing.js (new). Data: data/analytics.json rotated daily.
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Make 50+ requests through proxy, 2) Verify analytics.json has per-key/per-provider data, 3) Verify Analytics tab shows correct charts, 4) Verify cost estimates match expected pricing

tasks.title: Fallback provider routing
tasks.description: Enable cross-provider failover: when a provider's keys are exhausted, automatically retry the request on a configured fallback provider. Define fallback chains via env config: {PROVIDER}_FALLBACK=fallback_name and optional {PROVIDER}_FALLBACK_MODEL=model_id. Example: OPENAI_GROQ_FALLBACK=openai_claude means if groq keys exhaust, try openai with the same payload. Admin UI to configure fallback chains per provider with a dependency visualizer.
tasks.details: Files: src/core/fallbackRouter.js (new — FallbackRouter class with chain resolution and retry logic), src/server.js (wire fallback into handleRequest after all-keys-exhausted), src/core/config.js (parse FALLBACK env vars), public/admin.html (fallback config section in Settings). Config: {TYPE}_{PROVIDER}_FALLBACK={fallback_provider}, {TYPE}_{PROVIDER}_FALLBACK_MODEL={model_name}. Max fallback depth: 2.
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Configure fallback: groq → openai, 2) Disable all groq keys, 3) Send request to groq, 4) Verify it falls back to openai, 5) Verify response comes from openai

tasks.title: Prometheus /metrics endpoint
tasks.description: Add a standard /metrics endpoint exposing KeyProxy statistics in Prometheus text exposition format. Metrics: keyproxy_providers_total (gauge), keyproxy_keys_active_per_provider (gauge), keyproxy_requests_total (counter with labels: provider, status), keyproxy_request_duration_seconds (histogram), keyproxy_key_rotations_total (counter with label: provider), keyproxy_errors_total (counter with labels: provider, type). No npm dependencies — build Prometheus format strings manually.
tasks.details: Files: src/core/metrics.js (new — MetricsCollector class with Prometheus text format output), src/server.js (add /metrics route, wire counters into request handler and key rotator), public/admin.html (no UI changes needed). Endpoint is unauthenticated (read-only metrics). Format: standard Prometheus text exposition format v0.0.4.
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Start server, 2) curl localhost:8990/metrics, 3) Verify all metric names present, 4) Make API requests, 5) Verify counters increment, 6) Verify format passes promtool check metrics

tasks.title: Circuit breaker for providers
tasks.description: Implement a circuit breaker pattern per provider to stop sending requests to consistently failing providers. Track consecutive failures. After threshold (default: 5, env: KEYPROXY_CB_THRESHOLD), open the circuit (requests get 503 immediately). After timeout (default: 30s, env: KEYPROXY_CB_TIMEOUT_SEC), transition to half-open (allow one probe request). If probe succeeds, close circuit. Wire circuit state into HealthMonitor status and admin UI. Notify via Notifier when circuit opens/closes.
tasks.details: Files: src/core/circuitBreaker.js (new — CircuitBreaker class with closed/open/half-open states), src/server.js (instantiate per provider, check before routing), src/core/healthCheck.js (report circuit state), public/admin.html (circuit status badge in Management tab, force-close button). Config: KEYPROXY_CB_THRESHOLD=5, KEYPROXY_CB_TIMEOUT_SEC=30.
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Simulate 5 consecutive failures for a provider, 2) Verify circuit opens, 3) Verify next request gets 503 immediately, 4) Wait timeout, 5) Verify half-open state, 6) Verify successful probe closes circuit

tasks.title: Import/export configuration backup
tasks.description: Add export/import functionality to backup and restore all KeyProxy configuration. Export generates a JSON file containing: providers, keys, env settings, notification config, health check config, fallback chains. Import merges or replaces current config. Useful for migrating between workstations or disaster recovery. Admin UI: Export button downloads JSON, Import button with file picker + merge/replace choice + confirmation. Sensitive fields (API keys) are optionally included or excluded via toggle.
tasks.details: Files: src/core/configExporter.js (new — export/import logic), src/server.js (2 API routes: POST /admin/api/export-config, POST /admin/api/import-config), public/admin.html (import/export section in Settings modal). Export format: { version, exportedAt, config: { envVars, providers }, includeSecrets: bool }. Import: validate JSON, merge or replace, write .env, reload.
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Export config, 2) Verify JSON contains all providers/settings, 3) Delete a provider, 4) Import with merge, 5) Verify provider restored, 6) Import with replace, 7) Verify clean slate

tasks.title: Per-key RPM tracking and rate awareness
tasks.description: Track requests-per-minute per API key using a sliding window counter (last 60 seconds). Display current RPM in the admin UI next to each key. Add optional warning badges when a key approaches known provider rate limits (e.g., OpenAI free tier: 3 RPM, Gemini free: 15 RPM). Display as a heatmap bar in the key table (green → yellow → red). No enforcement — purely informational to help users distribute load. Configurable provider rate limit presets in src/core/rateLimits.js.
tasks.details: Files: src/core/rateTracker.js (new — SlidingWindowCounter class), src/core/keyRotator.js (increment tracker on each request), src/server.js (API route for per-key RPM data), public/admin.html (RPM badge in key table, tooltip with provider limit). Rate limit presets: OpenAI free=3, OpenAI paid=500, Gemini free=15, Groq=30, etc.
tasks.testStrategy: 1) Make 10 rapid requests with same key, 2) Verify RPM counter shows ~10, 3) Wait 60s, 4) Verify RPM drops to 0, 5) Verify UI shows correct RPM badge color
